result.summary: Built the Tier-0 deterministic (no-LLM) per-card evidence-card screen exactly to Sol's spec. It reuses the mandated enforcement points rather than reinventing them: provenance.Graph.verify_span and resolve_attribution for structure/binding, report_ast._binding_from_card as the card->binding adapter, report_ast.quantities_in/number_stands_alone plus evidence_miner.number_tokens for mechanical numeric fidelity, and evidence_miner.derive_claim/is_complete/REGISTRY for cache recompute. Seven dimensions run per card (structure/schema, binding, caches+counts, numeric tokens, positive CoT content-class, facet-in-taxonomy, corroborator-binding), each returning a typed DimensionResult with a stable reason code; a dimension it cannot decide offline routes to NEEDS_OPUS (never a fabricated PASS) and an unpinned contract to NEEDS_CONTRACT. Generality is honored — no task-72/DOI/subject/venue literals anywhere; every rule fires on structure. The legacy scripts/quarantine.py is deliberately not imported. The schema lives at Sol's pinned path src/schemas/evidence_card_audit.py but is loaded by path via card_audit/audit_schema.py to bypass that package's pre-existing broken __init__.py (it imports a nonexistent POLARIS phase_models). Acceptance: 14/14 hermetic tests pass (clean card, determinism/metamorphic id-rename, and one-fault-per-dimension adversaries plus a compound canary caught in binding+CoT+numeric+facet). As a live sanity check I ran the screen read-only over the current 521-card corpus and real provenance_graph.json: it detected exactly the defects the plan predicted (67 duplicate-id collisions, 9 incomplete corroborator bindings, 9 inflated source counts, 3 unit swaps), with no primary-binding failures. No forbidden files were modified (evidence_miner/provenance/event_ledger/report_ast/composer untouched), the mine/recovery/outputs/ledger were not touched, no Opus calls were made (correct for T0), and no git commit was made.

Built the Opus semantic audit harness at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/card_audit/harness.py per Sol's plan (§3 Tier 1/2/3, §Faithfulness, §Numeric, §Relevance, §Voice, §4, Phase 2/3/8). Faithfulness is NOT reinvented: it reuses report_ast.entailed_by_span on the exact verified span bytes (resolved via RA._binding_from_card + graph.verify_span, then manifestation.text[start:end]) against the canonically-normalized claim, and treats a report-AST FAIL as decisive and non-overridable by any Opus opinion; corroborators must independently entail the primary claim (no laundering). It defines the closed Opus structured-verdict JSON schema plus a dependency-free fail-closed validator (rejects unknown keys, wrong audit_row_id, illegal verdicts, missing numeric subdimensions, illegal dispositions), an injectable opus-only transport (subprocess_opus_runner shells `claude -p --model opus --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --output-format json --json-schema`, retries same model only, no Sonnet/Haiku/GLM fallback), model-metadata enforcement (model_is_opus proves opus and rejects cheaper models), self-contained generality-safe prompts (only injected question/facets/span/receipt, zero task-72/DOI/venue literals), the Tier-1 pass A / Tier-2 pass B runner, a fail-closed combination that routes disagreements and Opus-alleged-atoms to Tier 3, a Tier-3 adjudicator that can overturn an Opus false positive but never lift a Tier-0 or report-AST failure, and voice normalization (every v2 card ATTRIBUTED, ATTRIBUTED->OWNED laundering of an unreachable card blocked). Added tests/test_card_audit_harness.py (28 hermetic tests, deterministic judge + injected Opus runner stub, no real model call) — all 28 pass, and the existing Tier-0 suite (14) still passes. No real Opus batch was run (that is the ADVERSARY phase; the mine is still active). Protected files (evidence_miner.py, provenance.py, event_ledger.py, report_ast.py) and the running mine/outputs were not touched; no git commit.

Built the DISPOSITION engine at scripts/card_audit/disposition.py, consuming the Tier-0 receipt and the harness CombinedVerdict and turning each proposed disposition into exactly one accounted outcome per Sol §4. All seven verbs are implemented with their rules: KEEP_UNCHANGED ships the card; REPAIR_TIGHTEN applies TYPED field changes only (source bytes/hash/manifestation/offsets immutable via IMMUTABLE_FIELDS, derived caches recomputed, claim recomputed via evidence_miner.derive_claim never model prose), then RE-RUNS the full deterministic screen (tier0.screen_card) plus report-AST faithfulness plus, when an Opus runner is injected, the whole harness ladder; REBASE_TO_VALID_SUPPORT rebuilds a complete binding from an independently-entailing corroborator's graph bytes with a collision-safe id and recomputes attribution/counts from the live graph; REMOVE_BAD_SUPPORT_EDGE retains the primary, quarantines the bad edge with its reason, and recomputes n_sources/n_evidence_units; DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION gates entirely on report_ast.validate_node(Owned(...)) and routes to owned_suggestions (not audited_cards), with harness.voice_launder_blocked refusing the ATTRIBUTED->OWNED launder; QUARANTINE_CARD/QUARANTINE_SUPPORT_EDGE. Any repair that does not hold on rerun falls closed to quarantine, and reconcile_corpus enforces the Phase-4 accounting invariant (input_top_level = kept + repaired + quarantined + demoted; input_support_edges = kept + repaired + quarantined) so nothing is silently dropped. Reuses the pinned enforcement points (derive_claim, screen_card, screen_support_edge, _verified_evidence_units, audit_faithfulness_primary/audit_card, validate_node); does NOT import legacy scripts/quarantine.py; carries no task-72/DOI/subject/venue literal (a generality test greps the module). The acceptance test (22 cases) passes, and the full card_audit suite (tier0 + harness + disposition, 64 tests) passes. evidence_miner.py, provenance.py, report_ast.py, event_ledger.py and the running mine/outputs were not touched. No git commit made.

Built the ADVERSARY phase for Sol's evidence-card audit exactly to spec, reusing the already-built Tier-0 screen (card_audit.tier0), the Opus harness (card_audit.harness, which reuses report_ast.entailed_by_span for faithfulness), and the disposition engine (card_audit.disposition); the legacy scripts/quarantine.py is not imported and the mine/forbidden files (evidence_miner, provenance, event_ledger, report_ast) are untouched. tests/test_card_audit_adversary.py (28 tests, all pass) seeds the KNOWN-BAD cards Sol requires and asserts the audit catches each END TO END (tier0 -> harness -> dispose): a compound canary carrying a fabricated binding + CoT scaffold + numeric sign-flip + off-topic content is caught in all four dimensions (structure/binding + CoT deterministically, sign-flip via the Opus numeric.direction subdimension, off-topic via Opus relevance), disposed to QUARANTINE, and never yields a clean card; four single-fault canaries are each caught by only their intended dimension; plus additional attacks (wrong hash, source-policy laundering, percent/pp swap, duplicate id, incomplete + false-but-verifying corroboration, Opus modality/population/geography/precision/uncertainty violations, transport/garbage/unverified-model fail-closed, and the ATTRIBUTED->OWNED launder block). Two load-bearing-check proofs (monkeypatching _dim_binding/_dim_cot to no-ops) and two generality guards (no task literal in the rule template or modules) round it out. New module scripts/card_audit/judge_guard.py closes the Phase-8 judge-error-sentinel gap: report_ast admits an ("ENTAILED","judge_error: ...") reply because _canon_verdict reads only the verdict token, so the guard wraps the injected judge and downgrades an ENTAILED-with-error reply to UNCERTAIN (fail-closed) without touching report_ast. I then validated the whole pipeline on real cards NOW via scripts/card_audit/validate_now.py (read-only): Tier-0 ingested real recovered/v2 cards against the real 437-manifestation graph without crashing, and a SMALL real opus batch (2 cards, --model opus --effort max) returned Opus-proven, schema-valid verdicts ($0.60-0.77/card, ~130s each), proving the opus audit path works; I then STOPPED (did not run the full audit — the real cards are still being mined). IMPORTANT: proving the opus path surfaced two production-transport defects in the already-built harness that would have made the audit fail-closed on EVERY real card, which I fixed surgically in card_audit/harness.py: (1) subprocess_opus_runner passed --json-schema a file path, but this CLI (v2.1.208) requires the schema inline (a path is parsed as JSON and rejected, rc=1); (2) model_is_opus rejected every real envelope because claude -p bills a tiny auxiliary haiku housekeeping turn alongside the substantive opus result — it now requires opus to be the dominant-output-token model (still rejects sonnet/haiku/glm-only and haiku-does-the-work decoys; all prior harness tests still pass).</parameter>
